contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 20
- Description: truesight_autopilot — fix duplicate CORS header on /oracle-advisory (PR #86)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_autopilot/pull/86
  
  Details:
  Gary reported CORS error on /oracle-advisory from the oracle.truesight.me frontend. Diagnosed: response carried TWO Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers — one per-origin from FastAPI's CORSMiddleware (correct wildcard+credentials behavior, echoes origin with Vary), one '*' from nginx's add_header (intended as fallback). CORS spec forbids duplicate ACAO; browser rejects.
  
  PR #86: strip ALL CORS-related add_header directives from config/nginx/sophia.conf, plus the OPTIONS-204 if-block short-circuit. FastAPI's CORSMiddleware handles preflight + method-bearing responses both. Validated against real prod nginx via temp symlink + nginx -t before merge.
  
  After self-deploy: curl -H 'Origin: https://oracle.truesight.me' on /oracle-advisory shows exactly 1 access-control-allow-origin header (was 2). Vary: Origin and Allow-Credentials: true correctly present.
  
  Trade-off: pure nginx errors (502/504/413) won't carry CORS headers. Acceptable — those are rare + operator-visible. The right pattern for nginx-side CORS-when-needed is more_set_headers from nginx-extras (overrides not appends), not add_header.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 33.33
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
